title: Proclamation No. 181, s. 1937
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 181

RESERVING FOR SCHOOL PURPOSES SIX PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF ARACELI, PROVINCE OF PALAWAN.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for school purposes, under the administration of the Director of Education, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following-described parcels of the public domain, situated in the municipality of Araceli, Province of Palawan, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232 and 232-A, to wit:

Lot No. 1 (barrio of San Juan, school site). —Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, N. 84° 19′ E. 45.92 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 12, barrio of San Juan, municipality of Araceli, thence S. 13° 03′ E. 83.51 m. to point 2; S. 84° 58′ W. 77.45 m. to point 3; N. 75° 16′ W. 40.44 m . to point 4; N. 21° 13′ W. 54.86 m. to point 5; N. 77° 10′ E. 120.26 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 8,489 square meters.

Points 1, 2, and 4, B. L. concrete monuments; points 3 and 5, nails crosses on sides of dolo fence posts. .

Bounded on all sides by streets.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 06′ E.

Points referred to marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, sheet No. 1.

Surveyed, September 17, 1931. Approved, July 20, 1932.

Lot No. 2 (barrio of Bacao, school site). —Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, S. 71° 56′ E. 2,641.38 m., more or less, from B. L. B. M. No. 1, barrio of Bacao, municipality of Araceli, thence N. 35° 40′ E. 73.18 m. to point 2; N. 33° 22′ E. 58.48 m. to point 3; S. 47? 46′ E. 41.10 m. to point 4; S. 39° 15′ W. 58.41 m. to point 5; S. 48° 09′ E. 47.68 m. to point 6; S. 35° 12′ W. 64.25 m. to point 7; N. 53° 49′ W. 82.76 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 7,860 square meters.

Point 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7, B. L. concrete monuments; points 5 and 6, nails at tops ipil fence posts.

Bounded on the northeast, by property claimed by Valentin Sabinet; on the east, by properties claimed by Fermina Gregorio and Valentin Sabinet; on the southwest, by road and property claimed by Cristobal Arguelles; and on the northwest, by property claimed by Juan Rosel.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 37′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, sheet No. 2.

Surveyed, September 18, 1931. Approved, July 20, 1932.

Lot No. 3 (barrio of Dumaran, school site). —Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, S. 81° 37′ E. 18.17 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 2, barrio of Du­maran, municipality of Araceli, thence N. 77° 14′ E. 29.10 m. to point 2; N. 88° 03′ E. 44.90 m. to point 3; S. 13° 14′ W. 48.90 m. to point 4; N. 57° 34′ W. 44.80 m. to point 5; N. 57° 13′ W. 28.83 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 1,825 square meters.

All points are B. L. concrete monuments.

Bounded on the north, by street; on the east, by property claimed by Pe Chuy Hue; and on the southwest, by street.

Lot No. 4 (barrio of Dumaran, school site). —Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, N. 77° 59′ E. 47.72 m. from B. L. B. M. No. 2, barrio of Dumaran, municipality of Araceli, thence S. 73° 26′ W. 48.93 m. to point 2; N. 3° 14′ E. 4.07 m. to point 3; N. 4° 13′ W. 29.07 m. to point 4; N. 69° 52′ E. 57.87 m. to point 5; N. 31° 24′ E. 23.84 m. to point 6; S. 84° 40′ E. 23.95 m. to point 7; S. 9° 57′ E. 25.51 m. to point 8; S. 4° 14′ W, 30.09 m. to point 9; S. 87° 27′ W. 44.02 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 4,007 square meters.

Points 1, 6, 7, 8, and 9, B. L. concrete monuments; point 2, tack cross on wall; point 3, B. L. B. M. No. 2; points 4 and 5, tacks crosses on trees; points 4, 5, and 6, on shore of China Sea.

Bounded on the north, by China Sea and property claimed by Pe Chuy Hue; on the east, by property claimed by Pe Chuy Hue; on the south, by street; and on the west, by public land.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 10′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232, sheet No. 3.

Surveyed, September 16, 1931. Approved, July 20, 1932.

Lot No. 1 (barrio of Tudela, Island of Calandagan). — Beginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232-A, S. 28° 11′ W. 1,326.39 m., more or less, from C. & G. S. triangulation station “Po,” Calandangan Island, thence S. 48° 14′ E. 43.86 m. to point 2; S. 48° 22′ E. 51.16 m. to point 3; S. 42° 01′ W. 67.42 m. to point 4; N. 45° 20′ W. 33.49 m. to point 5; N. 48° 41′ W. 49.44 m. to point 6; N. 31° 36′ E. 67.06 m. to point 1, point of beginning. Containing an area of 5,880 square meters. All corners are B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm. Bounded on the northeast, by properties of Tomas Vi-gonte and Jose Calanday; on the southeast, by property of heirs of Simeon Abrina; on the southwest, by properties of Jacinto Sacamay and Simeon Sacamay; and on the north-west, by barrio street.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 14′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232-A, sheet No. 1.

Surveyed: July 23, 1934. Approved: March 12, 1936.

Lot No. 2 (barrio of Centro, Island of Dumaran). —Be­ginning at a point marked “1” on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232-A, N. 70° 40′ E. 366.11 m., more or less, from B. L. B. M. No. 2, barrio of Centro, municipality of Araceli, thence N. 2° 00′ E. 20.91 m. to point 2; N. 66° 17′ E. 9.27 m. to point 3; N. 80° 17′ E. 93.77 m. to point 4; S. 9° 36′ E. 8.22 m. to point 5; S. 0° 53′ W. 107.25 m. to point 6; N. 64° 01′ W. 11.32 m. to point 7; N. 0° 20′ E. 98.48 m. to point 8; S. 79° 06′ W. 76.02 m. to point 9; S. 4° 01′ W. 80.83 m. to point 10; N. 81° 25′ W. 15.00 m. to point 11; N. 2° 56′ E. 58.72 m. to point 12; N. 3° 54′ E. 5.58 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 3,434 square meters. Point 7, old galvanized iron spikes in blaze in tree; points 8 and 10, old spikes in posts; and the rest are B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.

Bounded on the north, by properties of Santos Reynoso and Segundo Blanco; on the east, by properties of Segundo Blanco, Pascual Madrigal, and the Government of the Settlement of Dumaran; on the south, by Sulu Sea, property of the government of the settlement of Dumaran, and Sulu Sea; and on the west, by properties, of the government of the settlement of Dumaran, Simon Arzaga, barrio street, and property of Nicolas Reynoso.

Bearings true. Declination, 1° 17′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Mr-232-A, sheet No. 2.

Surveyed: March 12, 1934.

Approved: March 12, 1936.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this twenty-seventh day of August, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-seven, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines. the second.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: ELPIDIO QUIRINO Secretary of the Interior

Source : Legislative Library, House of Representatives
